authoritySource: Dr. Ahmed Hesham — OET Vocabulary assessment criteria for healthcare English

criteria.title: Clinical Accuracy
criteria.description: Definitions must be medically correct and reflect current clinical usage. No outdated terminology.

criteria.title: Register & Professional Fit
criteria.description: Entries must be appropriate for OET register: formal, healthcare-specific, non-colloquial, no trademarks.

criteria.title: Concision
criteria.description: Definitions ≤ 25 words; example sentences a single focused sentence in OET letter register.

criteria.title: Context Richness
criteria.description: Example sentences show the term in an authentic clinical context; synonyms/collocations genuine and common.

criteria.title: Pronunciation & Audio
criteria.description: IPA pronunciation (where applicable) follows British Received Pronunciation conventions; audio is clear, non-accented, 8–16 kHz mono Opus or MP3.

providers.medicine: Medicine — covers general practice, internal medicine, surgery, paediatrics, obstetrics & gynaecology terms.
providers.nursing: Nursing — covers clinical nursing procedures, handover language, documentation, and patient education terms.
providers.dentistry: Dentistry — covers oral anatomy, restorative procedures, periodontics, paediatric dentistry, and orthodontics.
providers.pharmacy: Pharmacy — covers dosage forms, dispensing language, pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic vocabulary, and counselling phrases.
